title: L4: Game Creation
description: Build three progressively complex Scratch games applying variables, conditionals, and loops: a Pac-Man style game, an underwater game, and a game of your own choice.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now

Answer these five questions from iteration, variables and conditionals.
steps.js:
createMCQSet('gm-dna',[{q:'Which script makes a sprite chase another sprite called Player?',opts:[{scratch:'when green flag clicked
move (3) steps'},{scratch:'when green flag clicked
forever
  point towards [Player v]
  move (3) steps
end'},{scratch:'when green flag clicked
go to [Player v]'},{scratch:'when green flag clicked
if <touching [Player v] ?> then
  move (3) steps
end'}],correct:1,feedback:'Correct - point towards and move inside forever creates chasing.',wrongFeedback:'A chasing sprite needs a forever loop containing point towards Player and move steps.'},{q:'Which script lets the right arrow move a sprite to the right?',opts:[{scratch:'forever
  if <key [right arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (5)
  end
end'},{scratch:'forever
  if <key [right arrow v] pressed?> then
    change y by (5)
  end
end'},{scratch:'when green flag clicked
move (5) steps'},{scratch:'if <touching [edge v] ?> then
  change x by (5)
end'}],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - x changes left and right.',wrongFeedback:'Right movement should change x by a positive number when the right arrow is pressed.'},{q:'Which script adds 1 to score when touching a Jewel?',opts:[{scratch:'forever
  if <touching [Jewel v] ?> then
    change [score v] by (1)
  end
end'},{scratch:'when green flag clicked
change [score v] by (1)'},{scratch:'forever
  move (5) steps
end'},{scratch:'if <touching [Jewel v] ?> then
  set [score v] to (1)
end'}],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - the score changes only when the touching condition is true.',wrongFeedback:'The touching check needs to be inside a loop, with change score by 1 inside the if.'},{q:'Which block resets a collectible to a random position?',opts:[{scratch:'go to x: (pick random (-200) to (200)) y: (pick random (-150) to (150))'},{scratch:'move (10) steps'},{scratch:'point towards [Player v]'},{scratch:'say [Got you!] for (1) seconds'}],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - pick random chooses a new position.',wrongFeedback:'Use go to x/y with pick random values to move the collectible somewhere new.'},{q:'Which block decreases the score by one?',opts:[{scratch:'change [score v] by (-1)'},{scratch:'change [score v] by (1)'},{scratch:'set [score v] to (0)'},{scratch:'show variable [score v]'}],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - changing by -1 subtracts one.',wrongFeedback:'A negative change reduces the score.'}]);

steps.title: Key Game Techniques
steps.content:
Key techniques for all three games

Here are the patterns you will combine. Read them — you will use every one.

Player movement
forever
  if <key [right arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (5)
  end
  if <key [left arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (-5)
  end
end
Sprite chases player
forever
  point towards [Player v]
  move (3) steps
end
Collect and reset
forever
  if <touching [Player v]?> then
    go to x: (pick random (-200) to (200)) y: (pick random (-150) to (150))
    change [score v] by (1)
  end
end
Enemy catches player, score drops
forever
  point towards [Player v]
  move (2) steps
  if <touching [Player v]?> then
    change [score v] by (-1)
    say [Got you!] for (1) seconds
  end
end

steps.title: Game 1: Pac-Man
steps.content:
Game 1: Pac-Man

  1. Delete the cat. Paint a maze background (rectangle tool, two colours).
  2. Paint a Pac-Man: a yellow circle. Duplicate the costume and rub a wedge from the second. Resize it to fit the maze.
  3. Pac-Man script: use a forever loop containing four separate arrow-key if blocks. Each if block should change x or y by 5.
  4. Create a Score variable. Set to 0 on flag.
  5. Add a reward sprite (search Bananas or draw a dot). Script: use a forever loop. Inside it, add an if touching Pacman block; inside that, go to a random position and change Score by 1.
  6. Add a chaser sprite. Script: use a forever loop containing point towards Pacman, then move 2 steps.
steps.js: (function(){var NW=1100,NH=650;function sf(){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(!f)return;var w=f.parentElement,bar=w.querySelector('.y7-check-bar'),bH=bar?bar.offsetHeight:44,fs=w.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'),aW=fs?window.innerWidth:w.clientWidth,sc=fs?Math.min(aW/NW,(window.innerHeight-bH)/NH):aW/NW;f.style.width=NW+'px';f.style.height=NH+'px';f.style.transform='scale('+sc+')';f.style.marginBottom=Math.round(NH*(sc-1))+'px';f.style.marginLeft=fs?Math.max(0,Math.round((aW-NW*sc)/2))+'px':'0';}function tfs(){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame'),w=f.parentElement,b=document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn'),fs=w.classList.toggle('y7-fullscreen');b.textContent=fs?'✕':'⛶';if(fs){window._sy=window.scrollY;document.body.style.overflow='hidden';}else{document.body.style.overflow='';window.scrollTo(0,window._sy||0);}sf();}sf();if(window._sfR)window.removeEventListener('resize',window._sfR);window._sfR=sf;window.addEventListener('resize',sf);if(window._sfK)document.removeEventListener('keydown',window._sfK);window._sfK=function(e){if(e.key==='Escape'){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(f&&f.parentElement.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'))tfs();}};document.addEventListener('keydown',window._sfK);document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn').onclick=tfs;document.getElementById('y7-check-btn').onclick=function(){runStepRuntimeTest({kind:'keyMoves',key:'ArrowRight',axis:'x',direction:'positive'},'✓ Arrow key controls working!','Arrow key not moving the sprite — check the forever loop has an if right arrow pressed block with change x by (5) nested inside.');};})()

steps.title: Game 2: Underwater
steps.content:
Game 2: Underwater Game

  1. Add the Underwater 1 backdrop. Delete the cat.
  2. Add Diver1 sprite. Costumes tab, add Diver2. Diver script: use a forever loop with arrow-key movement blocks and next costume inside the loop.
  3. Create Score variable. Draw a Jewel sprite. Script: use a forever loop. Inside it, add an if touching Diver1 block; inside that, go to a random position and change Score by 1.
  4. Add Shark2. Script: use a forever loop containing point towards Diver1 and move 2 steps. Also inside the loop, add an if touching Diver1 block that changes Score by -1 and says [Yum Yum!] for 1 second.

steps.title: Game 3: Your Choice
steps.content:
Game 3: make your own

Find a game you like on Scratch.mit.edu and recreate it, or invent your own. It must use at least three of these features:

  • A background that fits the theme
  • A sprite that animates (next costume in a loop)
  • Arrow key player control
  • A score variable that increases
  • A chasing enemy (point towards + move)
  • A sprite that resets to a random position when touched

quizQuestions.q: Which script makes a sprite chase another called 'Player'?
options:
forever
  point towards [Player v]
  move (3) steps
end

if <touching [Player v] ?> then
  move (3) steps
end

quizQuestions.q: How do you make a collectible reappear in a random place when touched?
options: go to x: (pick random -240 to 240) y: (pick random -180 to 180)

quizQuestions.q: How do you decrease the score by 1?
Which script adds 1 to score when the sprite touches 'Jewel'?
options:
when green flag clicked
change [score v] by (1)

forever
  if <touching [Jewel v] ?> then
    change [score v] by (1)
  end
end

if <touching [Jewel v] ?> then
  set [score v] to (1)
end

quizQuestions.q: Build a sprite that chases the mouse pointer in a forever loop. Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer:
when green flag clicked
forever
  point towards [mouse-pointer v]
  move (3) steps
end

quizQuestions.q: Build a collectible sprite that jumps to a random position and increases the score when touched by another sprite. Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer:
forever
  if <touching [Player v] ?> then
    go to x: (pick random (-240) to (240)) y: (pick random (-180) to (180))
    change [score v] by (1)
  end
end

quizQuestions.q: Build a script with arrow key control: right arrow moves the sprite right, left arrow moves it left, all inside a forever loop. Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer:
forever
  if <key [right arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (5)
  end
  if <key [left arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (-5)
  end
end
